name: PAN Foundation Copay Assistance
eligibility: US residents with specific diagnoses who are underinsured. Income must be at or below 400% of the Federal Poverty Level.
description: Helps cover out-of-pocket costs for medications and treatments across dozens of disease-specific funds.

name: HealthWell Foundation Grants
eligibility: US residents who are underinsured and meet income criteria. Must have a qualifying diagnosis.
description: Provides financial assistance for prescription copays, premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance for underinsured Americans.

name: The Assistance Fund
eligibility: US patients with high out-of-pocket costs for specific chronic and rare diseases. Income-based qualification.
description: Copay assistance for patients with chronic and rare diseases who face high out-of-pocket treatment costs.

name: NORD RareCare Patient Assistance
eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with a rare disease in the US. Income-based qualification required.
description: Financial assistance for copayments, travel to specialists, and medical costs for rare disease patients.

name: Chive Charities Medical Grants
eligibility: Individuals with rare medical conditions, first responders, and veterans in the US. Application reviewed case-by-case.
description: Grants for life-changing medical equipment, treatments, and therapies for individuals with rare diagnoses.

name: MDPH Disability Compensation (PCH)
eligibility: French residents under 60 with a disability. Must apply through local MDPH office.
description: Prestation de Compensation du Handicap covers personal assistance, technical aids, home modifications, and transport costs.

name: Erasmus+ Health Mobility
eligibility: EU citizens and residents pursuing health-related training, education, or professional mobility across EU member states.
description: Supports cross-border mobility for health professionals and students, including training placements and knowledge exchange.

name: Federal Ministry of Health Research Grants
eligibility: German institutions, researchers, and healthcare organizations working on public health improvement projects.
description: Funding for health research projects, digital health innovations, and public health infrastructure in Germany.

name: NHS Individual Funding Requests
eligibility: UK residents registered with NHS who need treatments not routinely commissioned. Must demonstrate clinical exceptionality.
description: Allows patients to request funding for treatments not normally available on the NHS due to exceptional clinical circumstances.

name: State Health Programs (LEPL)
eligibility: Georgian citizens and residents. Covers various medical treatments including surgeries, medications, and diagnostics.
description: Government-funded health programs covering essential medical services, medications, and specialized treatments for Georgian citizens.

name: Child Neurology Foundation Family Support
eligibility: Families of children with neurological conditions in the US. Must demonstrate financial need.
description: Financial assistance for families navigating pediatric neurological conditions, covering therapy costs and supportive services.

name: MDA Care & Support Grants
eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with neuromuscular diseases in the US. Must be registered with MDA.
description: Grants for durable medical equipment, physical therapy, and rehabilitation services for neuromuscular disease patients.

name: CAF/AEEH — Disabled Children Allowance
eligibility: French families with children under 20 who have a disability rate of at least 50%. Assessed by MDPH.
description: Monthly allowance for families raising children with disabilities, covering rehabilitation, therapy, and specialized education costs.

name: CAMSP Early Intervention Centers
eligibility: Children aged 0–6 in France with developmental delays or disabilities. Referral from pediatrician or family doctor.
description: Free early intervention services including physiotherapy, speech therapy, and psychomotor therapy for young children.

name: Shriners Children’s Hospitals
eligibility: Children up to age 18 with orthopedic conditions, burns, spinal cord injuries, or cleft lip/palate. No insurance required.
description: Free pediatric specialty care including orthopedic rehabilitation, burn care, and spinal cord injury treatment regardless of ability to pay.

name: Global Genes RARE Impact Grant
eligibility: US-based rare disease patient advocacy organizations. Must be a registered nonprofit.
description: Grants to support rare disease organizations in advancing their missions through programs, research, and community building.

name: EURORDIS Rare Disease Grants
eligibility: European rare disease patient organizations and research groups. Must be EURORDIS members or partners.
description: Funding for rare disease research, patient advocacy, and cross-border healthcare access initiatives across Europe.

name: Orphan Disease Center Research Grants
eligibility: Academic researchers and institutions studying rare and orphan diseases. Peer-reviewed application process.
description: Pilot grants for innovative research projects focused on understanding and treating rare and orphan diseases.

name: RTW Foundation Ultra-Rare Grants
eligibility: Researchers and organizations focused on ultra-rare diseases affecting fewer than 2,000 patients worldwide.
description: Funding for research and drug development targeting ultra-rare diseases with limited existing treatment options.

name: FDA Orphan Products Grants Program
eligibility: Academic institutions, pharmaceutical companies, and researchers conducting clinical trials for rare disease treatments.
description: Federal grants supporting clinical development of products for rare diseases, including drugs, biologics, devices, and medical foods.

name: St. Baldrick’s Foundation Research Grants
eligibility: Researchers at academic institutions conducting pediatric cancer research. Must submit through institutional channels.
description: Funding for childhood cancer research including fellowships, scholar awards, and infrastructure grants.

name: Alex’s Lemonade Stand Foundation
eligibility: Researchers focused on pediatric cancer. Travel grants also available for families of children undergoing treatment.
description: Research grants and family travel assistance for childhood cancer, supporting both scientific discovery and patient families.

name: Courageous Parents Network Support
eligibility: Families of children with serious illness in the US. Open to all families regardless of diagnosis or income.
description: Free peer support, educational resources, and guidance for parents navigating their child’s serious medical condition.

name: Children’s Flight of Hope
eligibility: Children needing medical treatment far from home. Family must demonstrate financial inability to cover travel costs.
description: Free air transportation for children who need to travel long distances to receive specialized medical treatment.

name: Angel Flight America
eligibility: Patients (including children) who need air travel for medical treatment and cannot afford commercial flights.
description: Volunteer pilots provide free air transportation for patients traveling to medical facilities for diagnosis and treatment.

name: GITA — Innovation & Technology Agency
eligibility: Georgian startups and innovators with technology-driven projects. Must be registered in Georgia.
description: Grants for technology startups, innovation projects, and R&D initiatives in Georgia, including co-financing programs.

name: NSF SBIR/STTR — Digital Health
eligibility: US small businesses developing innovative digital health technologies. Must be a for-profit company with fewer than 500 employees.
description: Federal grants for small businesses conducting R&D in digital health, medical devices, and health IT innovation.

name: EIC Accelerator
eligibility: EU-based startups and SMEs with breakthrough innovations. Must demonstrate high impact potential and scalability.
description: Blended finance (grant + equity) for high-impact startups developing deep-tech and breakthrough innovations in the EU.

name: Horizon Europe — Health Cluster
eligibility: EU research consortia, universities, and companies working on health challenges. Must form cross-border partnerships.
description: Large-scale EU funding for collaborative health research and innovation projects addressing major health challenges.

name: Enterprise Georgia — Startup Funding
eligibility: Georgian entrepreneurs and startups. Must present a viable business plan and be registered in Georgia.
description: Government-backed startup grants and co-financing for Georgian entrepreneurs, including export support and business development.
